prediction.attackType: Server Software Component: Web Shell (MITRE T1505.003)
prediction.summary: An attacker from 10.20.8.77 successfully uploaded a web shell named 'shell.php' to the '/uploads/' directory of the web server. Following the upload, the attacker used the web shell to execute system commands, including 'whoami' and 'cat /etc/passwd', and attempted to download a secondary payload from 'evil.tld'. This activity was detected by WAF, IDS, and SIEM, which escalated the incident to a critical web shell compromise.

timeline.event: WAF alerted on suspicious PHP upload to webroot /uploads/shell.php from 10.20.8.77.
Attacker executed 'whoami' command via the web shell.
Attacker executed 'cat /etc/passwd' command via the web shell.
Attacker attempted to download 'stage2' from 'http://evil.tld/' via the web shell.
IDS detected web shell activity: repeated POST to .php with cmd parameter.
SIEM identified MITRE T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell.
ModSecurity blocked a SQLi probe from 10.20.8.77 to /uploads/shell.php.
SIEM issued a CRITICAL alert for webshell compromise on webserver01.

recommendations: Immediately isolate the compromised host (webserver01) to prevent further lateral movement or data exfiltration.
Perform a full forensic analysis of webserver01 to identify the initial compromise vector, persistence mechanisms, and any other malicious activity.
Remove the 'shell.php' file and any other unauthorized files from the server.
Block the attacker's IP address (10.20.8.77) at the perimeter firewall and review logs for other connections from this IP.
Review web server logs for any other suspicious activity from the attacker's IP or related to the 'shell.php' file.
Update WAF/IDS rules to proactively block similar web shell uploads and command execution attempts.
